model.label: MiMo-V2-Flash
model.description: MiMo • Flash-tier model configured through a custom endpoint for fast inference and lightweight agent tasks.

pick.rationale: Arizona enters as the superior team by a wide margin in efficiency metrics, ranking 3rd nationally in both KenPom and Torvik with a top-10 offense and elite defense, while Arkansas ranks 17th-20th with a potent offense but weaker defense. The Wildcats' balanced attack and defensive prowess should control the tempo and exploit Arkansas's defensive vulnerabilities, though the Razorbacks' offensive firepower gives them a credible upset path if they force turnovers and shoot well. Ultimately, Arizona's depth, recent form, and matchup advantage make them the reliable pick to advance.

reasoningStep.title: Team Quality and Efficiency Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Arizona holds a clear edge in overall team quality and efficiency metrics compared to Arkansas.
reasoningStep.evidence: Arizona ranks 3rd in KenPom and Torvik with a top-10 adjusted offense (127.2 KenPom, 126.2 Torvik) and elite defense (89.7 KenPom, 91.1 Torvik). Arkansas ranks 17th in KenPom and 20th in Torvik with a strong offense (127.6 KenPom, 127.8 Torvik) but a weaker defense (102.1 KenPom, 102.0 Torvik). Arizona's 31-2 record and wins over top teams like Houston and Iowa State further demonstrate their superiority.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

result.text: Arizona Wildcats Scores, Stats and Highlights - ESPN Skip to main content Skip to navigation ESPN NFL NFL NBA NBA NCAAM NCAAM NCAAW NCAAW MLB MLB NHL NHL Soccer Soccer More Sports More Sports Watch Watch Fantasy Fantasy Where to Watch Search 2 Arizona Wildcats 32-2 1st in Big 12 Home Schedule Statistics Roster Tickets External Link Men's Tournament Challenge NEW: TC Eliminator 2025-26 Schedule Postseason vs 16 Long Island 3/20 1:35 pm TNT Regular Season vs 3 Florida W 93-87 vs Utah Tech W 93-67 vs N Arizona W 84-49 vs 15 UCLA W 69-65 @ 3 UConn W 71-67 vs Denver W 103-73 vs Norfolk St W 98-61 vs 20 Auburn W 97-68 vs 12 Alabama W 96-75 vs Abilene Chrstn W 96-62 vs San Diego St W 68-45 vs Bethune W 107-71 vs S Dakota St W 99-71 @ Utah W 97-78 vs Kansas St W 101-76 @ TCU W 86-73 vs Arizona St W 89-82 @ UCF W 84-77 vs Cincinnati W 77-51 vs West Virginia W 88-53 @ 13 BYU W 86-83 @ Arizona St W 87-74 vs Oklahoma St W 84-47 @ 9 Kansas L 82-78 vs 16 Texas Tech L 78-75 vs 23 BYU W 75-68 @ 2 Houston W 73-66 @ Baylor W 87-80 vs 14 Kansas W 84-61 vs 6 Iowa State W 73-57 @ Colorado W 89-79 vs UCF W 81-59 vs 7 Iowa State W 82-80 vs 5 Houston W 79-74 Full Schedule Wildcats NCAAM Joe Lunardi's ultimate guide to men's March Madness 2026 21h Joe Lunardi Illustration by ESPN Men's tournament odds: Duke ahead of Michigan, Arizona to win tournament 21h Doug Greenberg Lance King/Getty Images SVPod: Scott Van Pelt and Stanford Steve are here to react to the 2026 NCAA Tournament March Madness bracket. Scott Van Pelt and Stanford Steve are here to react to the 2026 NCAA Tournament March Madness bracket. Steve gives his UNFILTERED thoughts on all the issues with the bracket. Duke's draw is TOUGH, and Steve is giving his Blue Devils NO CHANCE of reaching Indy. A potential Florida-Vandy rematch isn't ideal, and a road game in the Elite 8?? Plus, will chalk indeed fly? Is it Arizona's year? Which upsets does SVP like the most? Plus, a solution to the flopping problem, reaction to Bam Adebayo's 83-point game and an in-depth breakdown of Cam Young's Players Championship victory. Finally, thoughts on the nasty front taking over the east coast, Steve needs tickets to the Women's Final Four, an all-time SVP golf tactic and Steve's admission to grand theft auto. | SVPod Appoximate Time Codes/Topics (0:00) Intro (0:34) Bracket reaction (5:12) Should Miami (OH) have to play in Dayton? (7:37) Conference championship reactions (9:17) Steve’s issues with the bracket (10:42) Should Purdue be a 2-seed? (13:32) More bracket reaction (23:42) Bracketologists nailed it (25:57) The 6-line is LOADED (26:30) Watch out for USF (31:30) UCLA with a BIG road trip ahead (34:20) Which teams can play spoiler? (39:23) Ark-Wisc could be a banger (42:15) ALL the pressure is on Arizona (45:00) Reaction to Bam’s 83 piece (46:45) This year’s freshman class has been GREAT49:10 Final bracket thoughts (55:10) Women’s bracket is a JOKE (57:55) Anybody got ti

toolCalls.summary: Fetched https://www.espn.com/mens-college-basketball/team/_/id/8/arkansas-razorbacks and extracted 3000 characters of text.

result.text: Arkansas Razorbacks Scores, Stats and Highlights - ESPN Skip to main content Skip to navigation ESPN NFL NFL NBA NBA NCAAM NCAAM NCAAW NCAAW MLB MLB NHL NHL Soccer Soccer More Sports More Sports Watch Watch Fantasy Fantasy Where to Watch Search 14 Arkansas Razorbacks 26-8 2nd in SEC Home Schedule Statistics Roster Tickets External Link Men's Tournament Challenge NEW: TC Eliminator 2025-26 Schedule Postseason vs 13 Hawai'i 3/19 4:25 pm TBS Regular Season vs Southern W 109-77 @ 22 Michigan St L 69-66 vs C Arkansas W 93-56 vs Samford W 79-75 vs Winthrop W 84-83 vs Jackson St W 115-61 vs 4 Duke L 80-71 vs 6 Louisville W 89-80 vs Fresno St W 82-58 vs 16 Texas Tech W 93-86 vs Queens W 108-80 vs 8 Houston L 94-85 vs James Madison W 103-74 vs 19 Tennessee W 86-75 @ Ole Miss W 94-87 @ Auburn L 95-73 vs South Carolina W 108-74 @ 21 Georgia L 90-76 vs 15 Vanderbilt W 93-68 vs LSU W 85-81 @ Oklahoma W 83-79 vs Kentucky L 85-77 @ Mississippi St W 88-68 @ LSU W 91-62 vs Auburn W 88-75 @ 25 Alabama L 117-115 vs Missouri W 94-86 vs Texas A&M W 99-84 @ 7 Florida L 111-77 vs Texas W 105-85 @ Missouri W 88-84 vs Oklahoma W 82-79 vs Ole Miss W 93-90 vs 22 Vanderbilt W 86-75 Full Schedule Jaylen Waddle Trade Reactions + Titans GM Mike Borgonzi Peter offers his perspective and instant analysis on the Broncos-Dolphins trade that brings Jaylen Waddle to Denver and leaves question marks in Miami. Then, Peter details just how much there was to the A.J. Brown-to-the-Rams rumors from a week ago. After some careless, reckless, and completely absurd NCAA Tournament thoughts, Peter brings in Titans GM Mike Borgonzi. They talk about the Titans' recent spending spree, which included a slew of new players and faces, and 17 additions to the roster. They go through what Robert Saleh and Brian Daboll are looking for in players and why so many former Jets and Giants were acquired in recent days. It's Cam Ward storyline, after that, with Borgonzi detailing what he's seen in their franchise QB over the course of the last eleven months. Then, Borgonzi goes down memory lane and takes us through his Kansas City days and what he remembers from scouting Travis Kelce and Patrick Mahomes. Learn more about your ad choices. Visit podcastchoices.com/adchoices 15h Razorbacks NCAAM Hour 2: Overreaction Tuesday, plus ESPN’s Jay Bilas Previews the NCAA Tournament ESPN College Basketball Analyst Jay Bilas and Rich preview the 2026 NCAA Tournament including his prediction of Duke and Michigan losing in upsets, the Arizona Wildcats winning it all, why Arkansas’ Darius Acuff Jr. could steal the spotlight and take the Razorbacks on a deep run, reveals that he thinks America hates Michigan basketball because of Rich’ non-stop honking, and says why the pairing of Charles Barkley and Dick Vitale might be the greatest thing to happen to sportscasting in decades. Rich and Brockman debate which teams are likely to make the NFL playoffs next season and if the Patriots or Chargers will h

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
Bracket config id: demo-2026-bracket
Tournament year: 2026
Current game id: s16-west-1
Current game label: West Sweet 16 1
Round: Sweet 16
Resolved teams: Arizona (arizona) vs Arkansas (arkansas)
Prior committed picks: 54
